title: Exploring Science: Vocabulary Pack for Grades 3-5
description: This vocabulary pack introduces essential science terms for newcomer ELL students in grades 3-5, focusing on key concepts and the scientific process. Includes vocabulary list, mini-book, and interactive worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A child looking closely through a magnifying glass at a leaf.
A child in a lab coat mixing liquids in beakers with bubbles.
A thought bubble with a question mark and a lightbulb over a child's head.
A child pointing to a future event on a timeline, with a crystal ball nearby.
A clipboard with numbers and charts, next to a pencil.
A detailed drawing of a microscope with a slide underneath.
A person wearing a lab coat and safety goggles, holding a test tube.
A room with lab equipment: beakers, test tubes, Bunsen burner, safety shower.
A hand holding a ruler next to an object, showing measurements.
Different objects (e.g., fruits, vegetables, animals) being sorted into groups.
A child writing 'The answer is...' with a checkmark, looking satisfied.
A chart or graph showing the outcome of an experiment.
A magnifying glass highlighting footprints or fingerprints.
A hand adjusting a dial or slider, showing something that can change.
A complex web of interconnected ideas or concepts, like a mind map.
An apple falling from a tree towards the ground.
A lightning bolt, a lightbulb, and a sun, representing different forms of energy.
Illustrations of a solid (ice cube), liquid (water), and gas (steam).
A clear glass of water with dissolved sugar or salt, showing it's mixed evenly.
A variety of living things: a plant, an animal, and a microorganism.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the science word to its definition. Draw a line from the word to the correct definition.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une la palabra de ciencia con su definición. Dibuja una línea desde la palabra a la definición correcta.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the science word. Then, write the word again on the line below. Try to draw a small picture for each word.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza la palabra de ciencia. Luego, escribe la palabra otra vez en la línea de abajo. Intenta dibujar una imagen pequeña para cada palabra.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the picture that represents the science word. Then, write the word on the line.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea la imagen que representa la palabra de ciencia. Luego, escribe la palabra en la línea.

items.image_prompt: An apple falling from a tree.
A sun emitting rays and a lightning bolt.
A block of ice, a glass of water, and steam from a kettle.
A clear glass with water and dissolved sugar.
A small plant, a fish, and a tiny bug.

pages.text_en: Our Science Adventure! We are young scientists ready to learn.
pages.text_l2: ¡Nuestra Aventura Científica! Somos jóvenes científicos listos para aprender.

pages.text_en: First, we Observe the world around us. We look closely at plants and animals.
pages.text_l2: Primero, Observamos el mundo que nos rodea. Miramos de cerca las plantas y los animales.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: First, we Observe the world around us. We look closely at plants and animals.

pages.text_en: Then, we form a Hypothesis. This is a special guess about how things work.
pages.text_l2: Luego, formulamos una Hipótesis. Esta es una suposición especial sobre cómo funcionan las cosas.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Then, we form a Hypothesis. This is a special guess about how things work.

pages.text_en: Next, we Plan an Experiment. We will test our hypothesis in the Laboratory.
pages.text_l2: A continuación, planeamos un Experimento. Probaremos nuestra hipótesis en el Laboratorio.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Next, we Plan an Experiment. We will test our hypothesis in the Laboratory.

pages.text_en: During the experiment, we Measure carefully and collect Data. We write down all our findings.
pages.text_l2: Durante el experimento, Medimos con cuidado y recopilamos Datos. Anotamos todos nuestros hallazgos.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: During the experiment, we Measure carefully and collect Data. We write down all

pages.text_en: After the experiment, we look at our Results and find Evidence to support our ideas.
pages.text_l2: Después del experimento, miramos nuestros Resultados y encontramos Evidencia para apoyar nuestras ideas.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: After the experiment, we look at our Results and find Evidence to support our

pages.text_en: Finally, we Conclude what we learned. Science helps us understand everything, like Gravity and Energy!
pages.text_l2: Finalmente, Concluimos lo que aprendimos. ¡La ciencia nos ayuda a entender todo, como la Gravedad y la Energía!
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Finally, we Conclude what we learned. Science helps us understand everything, like

pages.text_en: Keep exploring! Every Organism and every piece of Matter has a story to tell.
pages.text_l2: ¡Sigue explorando! Cada Organismo y cada trozo de Materia tiene una historia que contar.
pages.image_prompt: a child-friendly illustration about: Keep exploring! Every Organism and every piece of Matter has a story to tell.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and understand 20 basic science vocabulary words in English and Spanish, and use them in simple contexts related to scientific inquiry.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce 3-5 words per day, using visuals and gestures. Read the mini-book aloud, pausing to discuss vocabulary. Use worksheets as independent practice or small group activities. Encourage students to create their own sentences with the words.
materials_needed: Printed vocabulary cards (optional, based on image prompts)
Magnifying glasses, simple measuring tools (optional, for hands-on observation)
differentiation_tips: **For beginning ELLs:** Pair students with a stronger English speaker. Provide sentence frames for verbal practice. Allow them to point to pictures for answers.
**For intermediate ELLs:** Encourage them to use vocabulary words in short sentences or describe the images. Have them define words in their own words.
**Extension activities:** Students can create their own 'science journal' using the vocabulary, or design a simple experiment using the scientific method terms.

answer_key.answers: Answers will vary as students trace, write, and draw. Teacher should check for legible tracing/writing and relevant drawings.
Answers will vary for coloring. Teacher should check for correct word written on the line.
